MULTIPLE TREES WERE REPORTED DOWN COUNTY WIDE. THE MOST CONCENTRATED DAMAGE WAS ON VALLEY CREEK ROAD WEST OF CULLEOKA WHERE NUMEROUS TREES WERE SNAPPED OFF ALOFT AND SIDING OF A ROOF OF A HOUSE TAKEN OFF. DAMAGE PATH ASSOCIATED WITH THIS ESTIMATED AT 300 YARDS WIDE AND ONE HALF MILE LONG.

TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN ABOUT 6 MILES NE OF CAMDEN AT 1040 A.M. CDT AND ENDED ABOUT 10.5 MILES NORTHEAST OF CAMDEN AT ABOUT 1047 A.M. CDT. MANY HUNDREDS OF TREES WERE SNAPPED/UPROOTED ALONG THE 4.5 MILE LONG PATH. DAMAGE WIDTH WAS 75 YARDS. TEN HOMES SUFFERED ROOF DAMAGE AND SEVERAL OUTBUILDINGS WERE DESTROYED. EF-1.

*** 2 INJ *** TORNADO BEGAN 8.3 MILES SSE OF HOHENWALD AND MOVED EAST-SOUTHEAST INTO FAR NW LAWRENCE COUNTY. HUNDREDS OF LARGE TREES WERE UPROOTED AND SNAPPED. HARDEST HIT AREA WAS A CAMPGROUND IN NAPIER WHERE SEVERAL LARGE CAMPERS WERE DAMAGED. TORNADO RATED EF-1 WITH WINDS UP TO 100 MPH. PATH LENGTH WAS 4.5 MILES AND WIDTH WAS UP TO 150 YARDS. TWO PEOPLE SUSTAINED VERY MINOR INJURIES.
NEAR NAPIER...TREES DOWN...TREE DOWN ON A DOUBLE WIDE...TRACTOR TRAYLOR BLOWN OVER
TREES DOWN THROUGHOUT THE SW PART OF COUNTY
ALONG HWY 166...BARN...POWER LINES AND TREES BLOWN DOWN IN NW GILES CO. CHURCH DAMAGED AS WELL.

TORNADO STRUCK NW GILES COUNTY 2.5 MILES NW OF CAMPBELLSVILLE OR ABOUT 13-14 MILES NW OF PULASKI. MANY LARGE TREES WERE UPROOTED AND SNAPPED. SEVERAL HOMES SUSTAINED MINOR ROOF DAMAGE. SEVERAL OUTBUILDINGS WERE DEMOLISHED. TORNADO RATED EF-1 WITH WINDS UP TO 100 MPH. PATH LENGTH WAS 1.1 MILES AND WIDTH WAS UP TO 100 YARDS.

TORNADO TOUCHED DOWN 4.1 MILES SW OF CROSSVILLE AND MOVED NORTHEAST TOWARDS THE DOWNTOWN AREA. NUMEROUS TREES WERE UPROOTED AND SNAPPED ALONG ITS PATH... WITH SOME HOMES SUSTAINING ROOF DAMAGE DUE TO FALLEN TREES. THE TORNADO LIFTED 9 TENTHS OF A MILE SOUTHWEST OF DOWNTOWN CROSSVILLE. THE TORNADO WAS RATED EF-1 WITH WINDS UP TO 100 MPH. PATH LENGTH WAS 3.2 MILES AND WIDTH UP TO 300 YARDS. THERE WERE NO INJURIES REPORTED.
TREES DOWN ON HOUSES...POWER LINES DOWM...INJURIES REPORTED
POSSILE TORNADO OCCCURRED RESULTING IN ONE MOBILE HOME BEING DESTROYED AND NUMEROUS TREES DOWN ACROSS HOUSES. A COUPLE OF BUIDINGS HAD THEIR ROOFS BLOWN OFF JUST NORTH NORTHEAST OF ALLARDT.
POSSIBLE TORNADO OCCURRED EAST NORTH EAST OF JAMESTOWN IN THE TOM PRICE ROAD AREA. ONE MOBILE HOME DESTROYED AND NUMEROUS TREES DOWN. COUPLE OF ROOFS OFF OF BUSINESS JUST NORTH NORTH EAST OF ALLDART.
